cards.title: Can Your Daughter Succeed Without a Tutor?
cards.body: A parent worries about affording tutoring for their bright 8-year-old seeking grammar school entry. An advice columnist suggests the parent may be projecting their own school anxieties onto their daughter, who has parental support as her real advantage.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Weird or Selfish
cards.body: Persistent prejudices paint only children as lonely and maladjusted, along with their parents for having just one child. The writer argues these stereotypes are outdated as more families choose to have a single child.

cards.title: Should You Start a Pension for Your Baby?
cards.body: Parents are being encouraged to open self-invested personal pensions for their children, adding to parental guilt. The columnist questions where families already struggling to save for their own retirement would find the money.

cards.title: Not All Screen Time Damages Children's Development
cards.body: A screen that responds to children can teach them to think critically, while passive screens may discourage thinking. The type of screen interaction matters significantly for child cognitive development.

cards.title: How Your Childhood Affects Your Self-Discipline
cards.body: Struggling with self-discipline isn't a character flaw but may stem from unmet needs during childhood. Building discipline now requires understanding what you didn't receive growing up.

cards.title: Music and Movement Build Family Connection
cards.body: A father used music and steady presence to help his three daughters find their own rhythms. This approach created both individual expression and a shared family beat.

cards.title: Intergenerational Trauma Can Be Healed
cards.body: The experiences of incarcerated Japanese Americans during WWII reveal how trauma passes through generations. Understanding this process helps families develop healing and collective care strategies.

cards.title: Hoarding Risk Is 50% Genetic in Women
cards.body: A landmark twin study shows genes account for roughly half of hoarding disorder risk among women, with family environment contributing little. Individual life experiences account for the remaining risk factors.

cards.title: Nursery Worker Abuse Could Have Been Prevented
cards.body: A staff member who raised concerns about paedophile nursery worker Nathan Bennett was ignored for months. The incident raises questions about safeguarding protocols in childcare settings.

cards.title: School Abuse Survivors Describe Disturbing Treatment
cards.body: Former pupils describe staff pelting them with footballs and forcing children to eat dog biscuits off the floor. Recent payouts have followed these revelations of institutional abuse.
